text:
Slovakian swimmer Tamara Potocka collapsed and was taken away by medical staff on a stretcher in front of fans inside La Defense Arena in Paris on Friday.

The 21-year-old was competing in the women’s 200-meter individual medley and finished seventh in her heat with a time of 2:14.20, which meant she did not qualify for the semi final.

After getting out of the pool, Potocka briefly sat beside the water before collapsing.

Medics rushed in to treat Potocka and gave her an oxygen mask. She was then moved on to a stretcher and carried away.

Slovakian team officials have confirmed that Potocka is asthmatic and had suffered an asthma attack.

She is now conscious and was able to communicate with doctors, but has been taken to hospital for further medical checks.

The head of Slovakia’s swimming team, Ivana Lange, said: ‘Tamara has asthma, and this whole combination of nervous and physical tension, while she did not have her spray immediately available, which she could use, contributed to the creation of this problem.

‘She received oxygen and necessary medication, her condition is constantly monitored.

‘A few minutes ago, the doctor told me that she must be monitored for several more hours. We will inform you about the current situation.’

The BBC commentator inside La Defense Arena said: ‘This doesn’t look good, 15,000 people are now watching an athlete surrounded by medical staff and I can assure you it’s absolutely silent in here.’

Potocka, who is competing at her first Olympics, was Slovakia’s third-best ranked swimmer last year.

title: Olympic swimmer Tamara Potocka collapses by pool immediately after race | Metro News
description: Slovakian swimmer Tamara Potocka collapsed and was taken away by medical staff on a stretcher in front of fans inside La Defense Arena
